headline: Morning Brief: Goldman rakes it in, but issues a warning

headline: Johnson & Johnson Stock Rises on Earnings Beat. These Segments Boosted the Quarter.
summary: Johnson & Johnson  stock gained on Tuesday after the pharmaceutical giant and medical device maker posted better-than-expected earnings and flagged strong sales growth in its first quarter.  J&J posted adjusted earnings of $2.70 a share for its latest quarter, narrowly beating out the $2.68 a share analysts had anticipated.  For the full year, J&J is targeting adjusted earnings of $11.45 to $11.65 a share, up slightly from a prior range of $11.43 to $11.63 a share.

content.title: Johnson & Johnson Stock Rises on Earnings Beat. These Segments Boosted the Quarter.

J&J posted adjusted earnings of $2.70 a share for its latest quarter, narrowly beating out the $2.68 a share analysts had anticipated.  For the full year, J&J is targeting adjusted earnings of $11.45 to $11.65 a share, up slightly from a prior range of $11.43 to $11.63 a share.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Wells Fargo, JPMorgan, BlackRock, American, Credo, CarMax, J&J, and More
summary: Stock futures were mostly higher Tuesday as investors gathered hope for a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran and big banks ushered in the earnings season.  First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

First-quarter net interest income came to $12.1 billion, missing analysts’ forecasts for $12.3 billion and marking a 2% decline from the previous quarter.  The world’s largest asset manager reported $2.2 billion in net income, marking a 46% jump from the same period last year.

headline: Johnson & Johnson Beats Earnings Estimates. Cancer Treatments Help Drive the Quarter.
summary: Johnson & Johnson  has reinvented itself several times in recent years.  J&J posted adjusted earnings of $2.70 a share for the first quarter, narrowly beating out the $2.68 a share analysts had anticipated.  Sales grew by more than 11% in J&J’s Innovative Medicine arm, which includes its oncology portfolio, and 7.7% in its medical technology division.
